triage.rationale: Repo cloned and README identifies the relevant files (integrations_worker/batch_retry.py, http_client.py, retry_policy.py). The issue describes a hot-loop when remote 401s begin mid-run — this suggests a logic/regression in the batch retry worker's error handling or backoff policy rather than infrastructure. Fixing will touch the worker, HTTP client, and retry/backoff policy and requires tests and a PR; this is not a trivial one-line change but also not a cross-service large effort, so scope=medium.
test_plan: Unit tests: simulate delivery handler receiving 401 and verify the worker marks delivery failed and continues without busy-looping.
Integration test: simulate many endpoints returning 401 mid-batch and assert worker enters exponential backoff or idle period instead of high CPU busy loop.
Load test: run the batch retry worker under a simulated high-failure scenario and measure CPU to confirm no spike.
Run full test suite: python -m pytest tests/ -v and lint: python -m ruff check .
After PR, deploy to staging and run end-to-end test with monitored CPU and logs; confirm SRE-observed pattern is gone.
proposed_plan: Read integrations_worker/batch_retry.py and integrations_worker/http_client.py to locate the loop and error-path handling (confirm file paths: /home/ubuntu/repos/finserv-issue-demo/integrations_worker/*).

Add/adjust logic to ensure non-retriable 4xx responses (401) cause: (a) per-delivery failure marking and skip remaining retries for that delivery, and (b) if many partner requests are returning 401 due to credential rotation, the batch worker transitions to a backoff/idle state instead of busy-looping.

Update or centralize retry policy in integrations_worker/retry_policy.py to treat mid-run auth failures as either short-circuit non-retriable errors or to use exponential backoff with jitter at the batch-run level when a large fraction of requests are failing with 401.

Add unit tests and an integration-style test to tests/test_batch_retry.py that simulates: a) single delivery 401 mid-run and b) partner credentials rotated causing many 401s mid-batch, asserting the worker does not spike CPU and respects backoff.

Create a PR with the change, run lint/pytest (README suggests python -m pytest tests/ and ruff). If CI runs, iterate until CI passes; include a clear changelog/PR description and link SRE logs if allowed.
missing_information: SRE logs referenced in the issue (full request/response traces and timestamps) — not provided in the repo clone.

Exact reproduction steps/environment: how the batch job is invoked in prod (concurrency, batch size, worker loop timing), and any relevant env/config used in production (retry limits, sleep intervals).
Whether 401 should be treated as globally non-retriable for that partner or per-delivery (policy decision).
Any feature flags or rollout constraints (can this be hotfixed directly to main or must go through staged rollout?).
Access to partner credential rotation timeline or secrets — to reproduce safely in tests (can be simulated).
